original_hypothesis.title: ILOManager Owner Can Set Refund Deadline to Far Future Permanently Locking Investor Funds
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ILOManager.setRefundDeadlineForProject has no constraints - the owner can set refundDeadline to type(uint64).max. Combined with a scenario where launch() fails (e.g., Uniswap V3 pool price changed), both the launch path (price mismatch revert) and the refund path (deadline never reached) become permanently blocked. All investor RAISE_TOKEN and project SALE_TOKEN are permanently locked in ILO pools with no recovery mechanism.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The refund deadline is the only fallback mechanism for failed sales. If it can be set to an unreachable value, the safety net is removed entirely.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setRefundDeadlineForProject has no bounds check; can set to type(uint64).max making refundable() modifier's block.timestamp >= refundDeadline unreachable

preconditions: ILOManager owner is malicious or compromised
Project has active ILO pools with investor deposits
Launch cannot proceed (price manipulation or other failure)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: No upper bound on refundDeadline parameter
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time before launch or refund is triggered

operational_questions: Is ILOManager owner a multisig or timelock?
Can refundDeadline be changed after _refundTriggered is set?
Is there any emergency mechanism to bypass this?
validation_requirements: Confirm no alternative withdrawal path exists

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: ILOManager owner sets refundDeadline to type(uint64).max for target project
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Manipulate Uniswap V3 pool price to prevent launch()
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Both launch and refund paths are permanently blocked
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: All investor RAISE_TOKEN locked with no recovery

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete permanent loss of all investor funds in affected pools
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: No on-chain recovery possible - requires contract migration or hard fork

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Before launch or refund is triggered

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Refund deadline set far in the future
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Owner modifying deadlines for active projects

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
